prompt: A vast, dark, organic neural network rendered as bioluminescent coral or tree roots spreading across black void. Each glowing node branches into many more connections than the filament that led to it — every point of arrival has fewer threads than the points it connects to. The network is asymmetric by design: following any strand always leads to a busier, more connected hub. The geometry is mathematically impossible — every local center is simultaneously peripheral to a larger center. Deep blacks, cold blue-white bioluminescent glow, hyper-detailed macro photography aesthetic, unsettling infinite depth, no text, no labels, no humans, no charts.
visual_logic: An organic bioluminescent network where every node has fewer connections than its neighbors — following any path always leads to a more connected hub, making every point simultaneously central and peripheral.
